processDescription: Assess a client’s financial capacity and payment security before committing to a bid or project. If financial concerns exist, establish payment security measures such as bonds or guarantees.

processTitle: Develop Target Materials List for Quantity Take Off Cost Tracking
processDescription:
Identify and document the target list of material items with the aim of capturing as much value in Quantity Take-Off cost tracking as practical. Target items must meet the following criteria:
•	The item is material in nature, as indicated in the Estimate;
•	The item is progressively modelled in 3D throughout the Design Phase; and
•	The item is modelled in 3D in a way that enables quantities to be extracted aligned with the breakdown of the Cost Estimate.

processTitle: Obtain NCC (National Construction Code) Performance Solution - Approvals
processDescription:
Compliance with the National Construction Code (NCC) is achieved by meeting performance requirements by implementing a deemed-to-satisfy (DTS) solution or by providing a performance solution. Deviations from DTS must be resolved with a performance solution which can take considerably longer to obtain approval and may require prototype testing to confirm performance.
Typically, Building Surveyors/Certifiers will assist with these approvals.

processTitle: Manage Latent Defects (post-completion of Defect Liability Period)
processDescription:
A Latent Defect is one that occurs after the end of the Defect Liability Period (DLP) but within the statutory obligation period. 
The definition of a latent defect is that it is not readily apparent but may become apparent at a later date. This can occur from a product, process (workmanship) or design failure. The definition and means of classification varies in different jurisdictions however the obligation remains. 
Latent defect management must follow the Latent Defect Procedure on iGMS.

Awareness of changes to the settled position, including the budget to build development, enables project risk profiles to be assessed and understood against the business portfolio.
Every change must be captured, initiated, reviewed, endorsed, approved and appropriately administered via the Change Request Notice (CRN) process.

processDescription:
Complete the commercial reporting process from month end COINS closed through to Project Review and submitting the PFR.

The scope of the process includes:
• Gathering the cost actuals and identifying inaccuracies
• Calculating the committed costs
• Forecasting future costs
• Updating margin improvement plan and risk and opportunity register
• Conducting internal project team reviews
• Participating in cost deep dives and Project Review
• Generating project PFR

processDescription:
For all Laing O’Rourke projects, Select Plant Australia is to be used as the contractor for all hire of plant and operators, with no exceptions.
